accepted.question_text: Let H be a Hilbert space over C and let A : D(A) ⊆ H → H be the infinitisimal generator of a bounded semigroup on H. Assume further that A−1 exists as a densely defined, closed operator. Is A−1 the infinitesimal generator of a bounded semigroup?

evidence.quote: Problem (Inverse generator problem). Let H be a Hilbert space over C and let A : D(A) ⊆ H → H be the infinitisimal generator of a bounded semigroup on H. Assume further that A−1 exists as a densely defined, closed operator. Is A−1 the infinitesimal generator of a bounded semigroup?

accepted.question_text: This problem, arising in the theory of Gaussian open quantum systems, consists in proving that a dissipative operator G which is quadratic in creation and annihilation operators (or, after a unitary transformation, a differential operator quadratic in partial derivatives ∂j and multiplication by coordinates xk ) generate an analytic semigroup. The solution has applications in the proof of strong positivity, irreducibility and regularity properties of Gaussian open quantum systems. Let aj , a∗k be the annihilation and creation operators on ℓ2 (Nd ; C) that are defined by closure from their action on the canonical orthonormal basis (e(n1 , . . . , nd ))n∈Nd , which is given by √ aj e(n1 , . . . , nd ) = nj e(n1 , . . . , nj−1, nj − 1, . . . , nd ), √ a∗k e(n1 , . . . , nd ) = nk + 1 e(n1 , . . . , nk−1, nk + 1, . . . , nd ). Let H, Lℓ be the closures of operators defined on the canonical basis by d d ζj ∗ ζ¯j X X ∗ κjk ∗ ∗ κjk H = Ωjk aj ak + aa + aj ak + a + aj , j,k=1 2 j k 2 j=1 2 j 2 d X Lℓ = (v ℓk ak + uℓk a∗k ) ℓ = 1, . . . , 2d, k=1 where Ω := (Ωjk )1≤j,k≤d = Ω∗ and κ := (κjk )1≤j,k≤d = κT ∈ Md (C) are d × d complex matrices with Ω Hermitian and κ symmetric, V = (vℓk )1≤ℓ≤2d,1≤k≤d , U = (uℓk )1≤ℓ≤2d,1≤k≤d are 2d × d complex matrices ζ = (ζj )1≤j≤d ∈ Cd . It is not difficult to show (see e.g. [38, Proposition 4.9]) that the closure of the operator G defined on the canonical basis by 2d 1X ∗ G = −iH − Lℓ Lℓ 2 ℓ=1 generates a C0 contraction semigroup P = (Pt )t≥0 on ℓ2 (Nd ; C). Suppose that the non-degeneracy condition (block-matrix form)  T   T V V V TU  V   K= V U = >0 U∗ U ∗V U ∗U holds, then sufficient conditions for P to be an analytic semigroup are also available. The problem it to find a classification of the set of parameters Ω, κ, U, V, ζ for which P is analytic. Alternatively, by the unitary correspondence of the above basis with multidimensional Hermite polynomials (multiplied by exp(−|x|2 /2) normalized), one can formulate the problem with differential operators 1 ∂ ∗ 1 ∂ aj = √ xj + , ak = √ xk − . 2 ∂xj 2 ∂xk In this case strict positivity of K implies     1 −1 1 1 K >0 1 1 −1 1 from which one P2dfinds the strong ellipticity condition for the self-adjoint part G0 = −(1/2) ℓ=1 L∗ℓ Lℓ of G d X U ∗ U + V T V − V T U − U ∗ V jk z j zk > ǫkzk2  Re j,k=1 for z = (zj )1≤j≤d ∈ Cd . Thinking of spectra of G0 and H one wonders if the semigroup P generated by P is analytic when K > 0 and H is bounded from below or from above.

evidence.quote: Problem (An analytic semigroup generation problem). This problem, arising in the theory of Gaussian open quantum systems, consists in proving that a dissipative operator G which is quadratic in creation and annihilation operators (or, after a unitary transformation, a differential operator quadratic in partial derivatives ∂j and multiplication by coordinates xk ) generate an analytic semigroup....

accepted.question_text: Let Ω be a Polish space. We call a semigroup T = (T (t))t≥0 of contractions on Cb (Ω) a transition semigroup if (i) for every f ∈ Cb (E) we have T (t)f → T (s)f locally uniformly whenever t → s and (ii) for every t ≥ 0 and every uniformly bounded sequence (fn )n∈N ⊂ Cb (Ω) that converges locally uniformly to f , we have that T (t)fn converges locally uniformly to f . Is there a characterization of the generators of such a semigroup akin to the Lumer–Phillips theorem?

evidence.quote: Problem (Lumer–Phillips for transition semigroups). Let Ω be a Polish space. We call a semigroup T = (T (t))t≥0 of contractions on Cb (Ω) a transition semigroup if (i) for every f ∈ Cb (E) we have T (t)f → T (s)f locally uniformly whenever t → s and (ii) for every t ≥ 0 and every uniformly bounded sequence (fn )n∈N ⊂ Cb (Ω) that converges locally uniformly to f , we have that T (t)fn converges locally uniformly to f . Is there a characterization of the generators of such a semigroup akin to the Lumer–Phillips theorem?

accepted.question_text: Let A and B be generators of C0 semigroups. Under which condition does C := A2 + B 2 generate an analytic semigroup?

evidence.quote: Problem 4 (Sum of squares). Let A and B be generators of C0 semigroups. Under which condition does C := A2 + B 2 generate an analytic semigroup?

accepted.question_text: Let (T (t))t≥0 be a not necessary strongly continuous semigroup on a Banach space X and consider the following condition. (i) (0, ∞) ∋ t 7→ T (t) ∈ L(X) is analytic. (ii) t 7→ T (t) is analytic on a sector containing R+ (iii) kT (t)k ≤ Metω , dtd T (t) ∈ L(X) and k dtd T (t)k ≤ Mt etω for some constants ω, M. Under which assumption added to (i), (ii) or (iii) does there exist a sectorial operator A generating (T (t))t≥0 ?
accepted.context_brief: Existence of a generator for analytic semigroups

evidence.quote: Problem (Existence of a generator for analytic semigroups). Let (T (t))t≥0 be a not necessary strongly continuous semigroup on a Banach space X and consider the following condition. (i) (0, ∞) ∋ t 7→ T (t) ∈ L(X) is analytic. (ii) t 7→ T (t) is analytic on a sector containing R+ (iii) kT (t)k ≤ Metω , dtd T (t) ∈ L(X) and k dtd T (t)k ≤ Mt etω for some constants ω, M. Under which assumption added to (i), (ii) or (iii) does there exist a sectorial operator A generating (T (t))t≥0 ?

accepted.question_text: Let A and B be the generators of two communicating C0 -semigroups on a Banach space and let G be the generator of the corresponding product semigroup. Find (the most general) conditions implying D(G) = D(A) ∩ D(B).

evidence.quote: Problem (Generator of product semigroups). Let A and B be the generators of two communicating C0 -semigroups on a Banach space and let G be the generator of the corresponding product semigroup. Find (the most general) conditions implying D(G) = D(A) ∩ D(B).

accepted.question_text: Let A and B be generators of C0 -semigroups on a Banach space X. (i) Define the sum C = A + B such that C becomes a (maximal) closed operator on X and Cx = Ãx + B̃x for all x ∈ X and some extrapolated operators A and B. (ii) Find assumptions on A and B such that C remains a generator on X, thereby unifying known perturbation results.

evidence.quote: Problem (A + B Problem). Let A and B be generators of C0 -semigroups on a Banach space X. (i) Define the sum C = A + B such that C becomes a (maximal) closed operator on X and Cx = Ãx + B̃x for all x ∈ X and some extrapolated operators A and B. (ii) Find assumptions on A and B such that C remains a generator on X, thereby unifying known perturbation results.

accepted.question_text: For unbounded linear operators A(t) on a Banach space X and for a starting time t0 , characterize the well-posedness of the non-autonomous Cauchy problem ẋ(t) = A(t)x(t) for t ≥ t0 x(t0 ) = x0 by Hille-Yosida type condition for an operator G generating an evolution semigroup on X = C0 (R, X) or X = Lp (R, X).

evidence.quote: Problem (Non-autonomous abstract Cauchy problems). For unbounded linear operators A(t) on a Banach space X and for a starting time t0 , characterize the well-posedness of the non-autonomous Cauchy problem ẋ(t) = A(t)x(t) for t ≥ t0 x(t0 ) = x0 by Hille-Yosida type condition for an operator G generating an evolution semigroup on X = C0 (R, X) or X = Lp (R, X).

accepted.question_text: Is a C0-semigroup with generator A immediately norm continuous if and only if ||R(is, A)|| tends to 0 as |s| tends to infinity?
accepted.context_brief: Characterization of immediate norm continuity by the resolvent

evidence.quote: Problem 9 (Characterization of immediate norm continuity by the resolvent). Prove or disprove that a semigroup with generator A is immediately norm continuous if and only if kR(is, A)k → 0 as |s| → ∞.
Comment (Charles Batty). This problem was answered negatively, by Ralph Chill and Yuri Tomilov in [21], and independently by Tamás Mátrai in [22].

accepted.question_text: Consider "non- C0 -semigroups", e.g., bi-continuous semigroups, and describe appropriate regularization properties.

evidence.quote: Problem (Regulariziation property of non-C0 -semigroups). Consider ”non- C0 -semigroups”, e.g., bi-continuous semigroups, and describe appropriate regularization properties.

accepted.question_text: Call a C0-semigroup T on a Banach space X asymptotically analytic if there exist an analytic C0-semigroup S on a Banach space Y, a bounded linear operator P from X to Y, and, for every f in X, an element g in Y such that T(t)f = S(t)g + w(t) for all t >= 0 with ||w(t)|| = o(||S(t)g||) as t -> infinity. Which C0-semigroups are asymptotically analytic? As a first step, what can be proved in the special case Y = X and P = I, and more generally which C0-groups governing second-order equations and which non-analytic semigroups governing functional differential equations are asymptotically analytic?

evidence.quote: We call S "asymptotically analytic" if there exist S, P as above such that from all f ∈ X there is g ∈ Y so that T (t)f = S(t)g + w(t), t ≥ 0, where kw(t)k = o(kS(t)gk) as t → ∞.

accepted.question_text: Let E := Lp (Ω, µ) for 1 ≤ p ≤ ∞ and a σ-finite measure space (Ω, µ) and let (Tt )t∈[0,∞) ⊆ L(E) be a strongly continuous semigroup on E such that Tt is a positive kernel operators for each t > 0, meaning that there exists a measurable function kt : Ω × Ω → R+ such that Z (Tt f )(y) = kt (y, x)f (x)dµ(x) Ω for almost every y ∈ Ω. Assume that (Tt )t∈[0,∞) is mean ergodic, meaning that the limit 1 T Z lim Tt f dt T →∞ T 0 exists in E for all f ∈ E. Does it follow that (Tt ) is strongly convergent, i.e. limt→∞ Tt f exists for all f ∈ E?
accepted.context_brief: Tauberian Theorem for Semigroups of Kernel Operators

evidence.quote: Problem 12 (Tauberian Theorem for Semigroups of Kernel Operators). Let E := Lp (Ω, µ) for 1 ≤ p ≤ ∞ and a σ-finite measure space (Ω, µ) and let (Tt )t∈[0,∞) ⊆ L(E) be a strongly continuous semigroup on E such that Tt is a positive kernel operators for each t > 0, meaning that there exists a measurable function kt : Ω × Ω → R+ such that Z (Tt f )(y) = kt (y, x)f (x)dµ(x) Ω for almost every y ∈ Ω. Assume that (Tt )t∈[0,∞) is mean ergodic, meaning that the limit 1 T Z lim Tt f dt T →∞ T 0 exists in E for all f ∈ E. Does it follow that (Tt ) is strongly convergent, i.e. limt→∞ Tt f exists for all f ∈ E?

accepted.question_text: Prove or disprove that the non-analytic growth bound ζ(T ) of a C0 -semigroup T coincides with the critical growth bound ωcrit (T ).

evidence.quote: Problem 13 (Growth bounds). Prove or disprove that the non-analytic growth bound ζ(T ) of a C0 -semigroup T coincides with the critical growth bound ωcrit (T ).

accepted.question_text: Let (T (t))t≥0 be a C0 -semigroup with growth bound ω0 := inf{ω ∈ R : kT (t)k ≤ M ω · etω for t ≥ 0} Find condition such that ω0 is a minimum, i.e., kT (t)k ≤ M0 · etω0 for t ≥ 0 .
accepted.context_brief: Precise growth behaviour in terms of the growth bound

evidence.quote: Problem 14 (Precise growth behaviour in terms of the growth bound). Let (T (t))t≥0 be a C0 -semigroup with growth bound ω0 := inf{ω ∈ R : kT (t)k ≤ M ω · etω for t ≥ 0} Find condition such that ω0 is a minimum, i.e., kT (t)k ≤ M0 · etω0 for t ≥ 0 .

accepted.question_text: Let (T (t))t≥0 be a bounded C0 -semigroup on a (complex) Banach space X, and let A denote its generator. Suppose that σ(A) ∩ iR = ∅ and that there exists α > 0 such that k(is − A)−1 k = O(|s|α) as |s| → ∞. Find β ∈ depending on the geometric properties of the space X (for instance its Fourier type, its type or its cotype) such that log(t)β/α −1 kT (t)A k = O , t → ∞. t1/α

accepted.question_text: Let E = Lp(Omega, mu) for 1 <= p < infinity over a sigma-finite measure space, and let (T_t) be a strongly continuous semigroup of positive kernel operators on E. If (T_t) is irreducible, meaning that for all nonzero positive f and g in E there exists t >= 0 with T_t f wedge g != 0, must (T_t) be expanding, meaning that T_t f > 0 almost everywhere for every t > 0 and every nonzero positive f in E?

evidence.quote: Assume that (Tt ) is irreducible, i.e. for all non-zero and positive f, g ∈ E+ there exists t ≥ 0 such that Tt f ∧ g 6= 0 (where ∧ denotes the infimum in the Banach lattice E). Does it follow that (Tt ) is expanding, i.e. Tt f > 0 almost everywhere for all t > 0 and all non-zero positive f ∈ E+ ?
Comment (Jochen Glück). The answer to the problem is negative, here is a counterexample:

accepted.question_text: Let E be a Banach lattice and let C : E → E be a positive quasinilpotent compact operator. Do there exist positive operators, A, B : E → E such that C = AB − BA with one of A and B compact?

evidence.quote: Problem 17 (Positive commutator problem). Let E be a Banach lattice and let C : E → E be a positive quasinilpotent compact operator. Do there exist positive operators, A, B : E → E such that C = AB − BA with one of A and B compact?

accepted.question_text: Use the Perron Frobenius spectral theory of positive C0 -groups to characterize unitary C0 -groups on a Hilbert space H that are unitarily isomorphic to a Koopman group on an L2 -space.

evidence.quote: Problem 18 (Characterizing Koopman groups on Hilbert spaces). Use the Perron Frobenius spectral theory of positive C0 -groups to characterize unitary C0 -groups on a Hilbert space H that are unitarily isomorphic to a Koopman group on an L2 -space.

accepted.question_text: Let M be a von Neumann algebra equipped with a normal, semi-finite, and faithful trace τ and p ∈ [1, ∞). Let (etA )t∈[0,∞) be an individually eventually positive C0 - semigroup on Lp (M, τ ). Is s(A) = ω0 (A)?

evidence.quote: Problem 19 (Stability of eventually positive semigroups). Let M be a von Neumann algebra equipped with a normal, semi-finite, and faithful trace τ and p ∈ [1, ∞). Let (etA )t∈[0,∞) be an individually eventually positive C0 - semigroup on Lp (M, τ ). Is s(A) = ω0 (A)?

accepted.question_text: Let (e^{tA}) be a C0-semigroup on a Hilbert space H, where -A is associated with a form a: V x V -> H with form domain V contained in H, and let C be a closed convex subset of H. Characterize, in terms of a and V, when C is individually eventually invariant under the semigroup and when C is uniformly eventually invariant under the semigroup.

evidence.quote: Problem 20 (Characterisation of eventual invariance by means of form methods). Let (etA )t∈[0,∞) be a C0 -semigroup on a Hilbert space H, where the operator −A is associated to a form a : V × V → H with form domain V ⊆ H. Let C ⊆ H be closed and convex. Give a characterisation, in terms of a and V , of one or both of the following properties: (a) The semigroup leaves C individually eventually i...

accepted.question_text: Let (Omega, mu) be a sigma-finite measure space, let V be a dense subspace of L2(Omega), and let a: V x V -> C be a closed sectorial sesquilinear form. Let A be the associated operator on L2(Omega), so that -A generates a bounded analytic semigroup (T(t)) on L2(Omega). If L-infinity-boundedness means that there exists M > 0 such that ||T(t)f||_infinity <= M ||f||_infinity for all f in L2(Omega) intersect L-infinity(Omega) and all t >= 0, can this L-infinity-boundedness property be characterized directly in terms of the sesquilinear form a?

evidence.quote: The semigroup (T (t))t≥0 is called L∞ -bounded, if there exists M > 0 such that kT (t)f k∞ ≤ Mkf k∞ , for all f ∈ L2 (Ω) ∩ L∞ (Ω).
Can L∞ -boundedness of (T (t))t≥0 be characterized in terms of the sesquilinear form a?

accepted.question_text: Let (Omega, mu) be a sigma-finite measure space, let V be a dense subspace of L2(Omega), let a: V x V -> C be a closed sectorial sesquilinear form, let -A generate the associated bounded analytic semigroup (T(t)) on L2(Omega), and assume that (T(t)) is L-infinity-bounded in the sense that there exists M >= 1 with ||T(t)f||_infinity <= M ||f||_infinity for all f in L2(Omega) intersect L-infinity(Omega). Must there exist a function g in L-infinity(Omega) with g > 0 mu-almost everywhere and 1/g in L-infinity(Omega) such that ||g T(t)f||_infinity <= ||g f||_infinity for all f in L2(Omega) intersect L-infinity(Omega) and all t >= 0?

evidence.quote: The semigroup (T (t))t≥0 is called L∞ -bounded, if there exists M > 0 such that kT (t)f k∞ ≤ Mkf k∞ , for all f ∈ L2 (Ω) ∩ L∞ (Ω).
It is clear that (T (t))t≥0 is L∞ -bounded if there exists a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) with g > 0 µ-a.e. and 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying kgT (t)f k∞ ≤ kgf k∞ for all f ∈ L∞ (Ω) ∩ L2 (Ω).

Problem 22 (L∞ -contraction for a weight problem). In the setting of Problem 21 assume that the semigroup (T (t))t≥0 is L∞ -bounded, i.e., satisfies (1) for some M ≥ 1. Does there exist a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying g > 0 µ-a.e., 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω), and (3)?

accepted.question_text: Let (Omega, mu) be a sigma-finite measure space, let V be a dense subspace of L2(Omega), let a: V x V -> C be a closed sectorial sesquilinear form, let -A generate the associated bounded analytic semigroup (T(t)) on L2(Omega), and assume that (T(t)) is L-infinity-bounded. Characterize exactly when there exists a function g in L-infinity(Omega) with g > 0 mu-almost everywhere and 1/g in L-infinity(Omega) such that ||g T(t)f||_infinity <= ||g f||_infinity for all f in L2(Omega) intersect L-infinity(Omega) and all t >= 0.
accepted.context_brief: Characterization of L∞ -contraction for a weight

evidence.quote: It is clear that (T (t))t≥0 is L∞ -bounded if there exists a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) with g > 0 µ-a.e. and 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying kgT (t)f k∞ ≤ kgf k∞ for all f ∈ L∞ (Ω) ∩ L2 (Ω).

Problem 23 (Characterization of L∞ -contraction for a weight). In the setting of Problem 21, can one characterize those L∞ -bounded semigroups (T (t))t≥0 , for which a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying g > 0 µ-a.e., 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω), and (3) exists?

accepted.question_text: Let (Omega, mu) be a sigma-finite measure space, let V be a dense subspace of L2(Omega), let a: V x V -> C be a closed sectorial sesquilinear form, let -A generate the associated bounded analytic semigroup (T(t)) on L2(Omega), and suppose there exists a function g in L-infinity(Omega) with g > 0 mu-almost everywhere and 1/g in L-infinity(Omega) such that ||g T(t)f||_infinity <= ||g f||_infinity for all f in L2(Omega) intersect L-infinity(Omega) and all t >= 0. How can one find or explicitly construct such a weight g?

evidence.quote: It is clear that (T (t))t≥0 is L∞ -bounded if there exists a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) with g > 0 µ-a.e. and 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying kgT (t)f k∞ ≤ kgf k∞ for all f ∈ L∞ (Ω) ∩ L2 (Ω).

Problem 24 (Weight construction for L∞ -contraction). In the setting of Problem 21, assume that a function g ∈ L∞ (Ω) satisfying g > 0 µ-a.e., 1/g ∈ L∞ (Ω), and (3) exists. How can we find or construct such a function g?

accepted.question_text: Consider the following properties of the generation A of a C0 -semigroups (T (t))t≥0 . (i) rg A closed. (ii) rg(1 − T (t)) closed for one t > 0. Does assertion (ii) imply assertion (i)?

evidence.quote: Problem 25 (Closedness of ranges). Consider the following properties of the generation A of a C0 -semigroups (T (t))t≥0 . (i) rg A closed. (ii) rg(1 − T (t)) closed for one t > 0. Does assertion (ii) imply assertion (i)?

accepted.question_text: Let (T (t))t≥0 be a contraction semigroup with generator A on a Hilbert space H. Consider the following properties: (i) There exists m ≥ 0 such that k(λ − A)xk ≥ m| Re λ|kxk for all Re λ < 0 and all x ∈ H. (ii) There exists m1 > 0 such that kT (t)xk ≥ m1 kxk for all t ≥ 0, x ∈ H. Does assertion (i) imply assertion (ii)?

evidence.quote: Problem 26 (Lower bounds). Let (T (t))t≥0 be a contraction semigroup with generator A on a Hilbert space H. Consider the following properties: (i) There exists m ≥ 0 such that k(λ − A)xk ≥ m| Re λ|kxk for all Re λ < 0 and all x ∈ H. (ii) There exists m1 > 0 such that kT (t)xk ≥ m1 kxk for all t ≥ 0, x ∈ H. Does assertion (i) imply assertion (ii)?

accepted.question_text: Characterize injective C0-semigroups via the backward uniqueness property, and apply the resulting criteria to non-autonomous Cauchy problems u'(t) = A(t)u(t) with sectorial A(t) by analyzing the corresponding evolution semigroup.

evidence.quote: Problem 27 (Backward uniqueness property). Study the ”backward uniqueness property”, i.e., characterize injective C0 -semigroups. Apply the results to the backward uniqueness property for non-autonomous Cauchy problems u′ (t) = A(t)u(t), where A(t) is sectorial, by examining the corresponding evolution semigroup. See Lunardi’s work on evolution equations in Banach spaces [5].

accepted.question_text: Does every bounded C0-semigroup on a Hilbert space have a bounded rational calculus?

evidence.quote: Problem 28 (Rational calculus and contractivity). (i) Does every bounded C0 -semigroup on a Hilbert space have a bounded rational calculus?
Comments. The answer to the first question is no.

accepted.question_text: When is a C0-semigroup on a Hilbert space similar to a contraction semigroup?

evidence.quote: Problem 28 (Rational calculus and contractivity). (ii) When is a C0 -semigroups on a Hilbert space similar to a contraction semigroup?

accepted.question_text: Let B be a Banach space. Let P be a projection on a finite-dimensional Banach subspace of B. Let L0 + λL be a generator of C0 -semigroup Utλ on B for all λ ∈ [0, λsup ). Let Ut0 leave both PB and its complement (I − P)B invariant, let PLP = 0. Denote Lt ≡ (Ut0 )−1 LUt0 . Let the integrals Z t Z tk−1 dt1 . . . dtk PLt1 . . . Ltk P, k = 1, . . . , n + 1 −∞ −∞ be finite for all t ⩾ 0. If is it possible to find such a λ-dependent operator r n,λ on PB , which is polynomial in λ, and λ-dependent semigroup un,λ t on PB, whose generator is polynomial in λ, such that P(U 0t )−1 U λt P = un,λ t r n,λ + O(λ2n+2 ), λ → +0 λ2 λ2 for all t > 0 ? Let us emphasize that we do not assume here asymptotic behaviour to be uniform in t, it is just assumed for each fixed t > 0. If there are counterexamples, what further restrictions should be assumed to obtain such an asymptotic estimate? Relevance. This problem is important for the derivation of perturbative corrections to Markovian quantum master equations. It seems to be a possible direction of generalization of the classical results by E.B. Davies to higher orders of perturbation theory in λ and seems to be held in a simple example discussed in . It also seems to be necessary for strict perturbative derivation of master equations recently obtained by A.S. Trushechkin .

evidence.quote: Problem 29 (A.E. Teretenkov). Let B be a Banach space. Let P be a projection on a finite-dimensional Banach subspace of B. Let L0 + λL be a generator of C0 -semigroup Utλ on B for all λ ∈ [0, λsup ). Let Ut0 leave both PB and its complement (I − P)B invariant, let PLP = 0. Denote Lt ≡ (Ut0 )−1 LUt0 . Let the integrals Z t Z tk−1 dt1 . . . dtk PLt1 . . . Ltk P, k = 1, . . . , n + 1 −∞ −∞ be finite for all t ⩾ 0. If is it possible to find such a λ-dependent operator r n,λ on PB , which is polynomial in λ, and λ-dependent semigroup un,λ t on PB, whose generator is polynomial in λ, such that P(U 0t )−1 U λt P = un,λ t r n,λ + O(λ2n+2 ), λ → +0 λ2 λ2 for all t > 0 ?

accepted.question_text: Let L be the closure on UC_b(R, R) of the differential operator (Lf)(x) = a(x)f''(x) + b(x)f'(x) + c(x)f(x), where a, b, c are bounded uniformly continuous real-valued functions and a(x) >= a_0 > 0 for all x. Find an explicitly given Chernoff function S(t) for L such that S is Chernoff-equivalent to the semigroup (e^{tL}), there exist a dense subspace D and constants M > 0, q > 1, n_0 > 0 with ||e^{tL}f - S(t/n)^n f|| <= M / q^n for all n >= n_0 and all f in D, and the formula for S(t) depends explicitly on a, b, c, f, and x without hiding the unknown semigroup inside the definition.

evidence.quote: Problem 30 (Super-fast converging Chernoff approximations example). Provide a meaningful example of Chernoff approximations that converge to a C0 -semigroup at least exponentially or prove that it is not possible in a meaningful setting.

The problem is to find a family (S(t))t≥0 of everywhere defined linear bounded operators in UCb (R, R) which has the following three properties: S and L satisfy the conditions of at least one version of the Chernoff theorem on approximation of operator semigroups; there exists a dense linear subspace D in UCb (R, R) and constants M > 0, q > 1, n0 > 0 such that ketL f − S(t/n)n f k ≤ M/q n for all n ≥ n0 and all f ∈ D; and S is defined by an explicitly given formula ... free from circulus vitiosus of any kind.

trace.notes: Mapped the paper as a curated list of numbered open problems in one-parameter operator semigroup theory. The contents page shows seven topical sections and Problems 1-30; no formal theorem/proposition/lemma index is present.
Marked q_009, q_016, and q_028a as solved because the source explicitly says they were answered or that the answer is negative; the remaining accepted items are left unsolved.
Saved exact JSON output to outputs/runs/2410.00416.json and updated outputs/latest.json.
